title: Letter XXXIX.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.text: A recommendation of one Amazonius, whose learning was much respected by Gregory.)
I wish well to all my friends.
And when I speak of friends, I mean honourable and good men, linked with me in virtue, if indeed I myself have any claim to it.

Therefore at the present time when seeking how I might do a kindness to my excellent brother Amazonius (for I was very much pleased with the man in some intercourse which has lately taken place between us), I thought I might return him one favour for all,—in your friendship and protection.

For in a short time he shewed proof of an extensive education, both of the kind which I used once to be very zealous for, when I was shortsighted, and of that for which I am zealous in its place since I have been able to contemplate the summit of virtue.
Whether I in my turn have appeared to him to be worth anything in respect of virtue is his affair.
At any rate I shewed him the best things I have, namely, my friends to him as my friend.

Of these I reckon you as the first and truest, and want you to shew yourself so to him—as your common Country demands, and my desire and promise begs; for I promised him your patronage in return for all his kindness.
